Pattern constructors for list, grid, and radial manifestations.
hexcasting.category.manifestation_intent: Interactive intent primitives for manifested spells.
hexcasting.page.manifestation.intro: I have long suspected that media and mind are not so separate as they seem. Only now do I begin to see how thought may be given form.$(br2)By constraining media with intent, I can compel it to manifest in the world around me. These manifestations may be perceived, and more curiously, they may accept further intent, carrying thought forward until it becomes action.
hexcasting.page.manifestation.splinters_: In trying to recreate Greater Sentinel, I first hypothosised that strong intent was the key. Instead I split away unstable slivers of my own will: brief-minded splinters that wait, cast once, and perish.$(br2)A splinter casts as me and draws on my media, yet its ambit is only sixteen blocks from its own position. If its cast mishaps, the splinter simply collapses rather than rebounding upon me.
hexcasting.page.manifestation.menus.crea: Takes a list of intentions, a menu title and manifests a list menu.
Takes a list of intentions, a menu title, and a number of columns to manifest a grid menu. Columns are limited to 1-10.
Takes a list of intentions and a menu title to manifest a radial button menu. Radial menus require button intents only; any non-button intent causes a mishap.
hexcasting.page.manifestation.intents_in: An Intent is an imposition of will on the media, shaping it into a vessel awaiting a spark of interaction to awaken it.$(br2)By themselves, intents are inert. A button waits for a press, an input for text, a numeric input for precise values, a slider for a nudge, a checkbox for a simple toggle, a selectable list for choosing iotas, and a dropdown for a selection. But once roused, they execute their patterns with the fervor of a cast hex.
hexcasting.page.manifestation.intents.in: Creates a button from a list of patterns and a label. When clicked, the button casts the patterns.
Creates an input field from a label. Entered text is pushed to the stack of an executed action, like from a button.
Creates a numeric input field from a current value and a label. Entered numeric text is parsed and pushed to the stack as a number when an action executes.
Creates a slider from min, max, current values, and a label. The chosen value is pushed to the stack of an executed action.
Creates a checkbox from a boolean initial state and a label. The state is pushed as 1 (checked) or 0 (unchecked) when an action executes.
Creates a selectable list from optional multi-select flag, a list of option iotas, max rows, and a label. On action execution, selected options are pushed as a single list.
Creates a section heading from a label.
Creates a dropdown from a list of options and a label. The selected option is pushed to the stack of an executed action.
hexcasting.page.manifestation.intents.pr: Formed from a position and direction vector. A focusing of the mind upon a place, impressed until presence is recognised there.
hexcasting.page.manifestation.intents.ma: Anchors a fragment of the caster's will to the impetus at the vector allowing it to cast some spells that would usually require the caster to be present. Can fracture mind up to 3 times.
Schedules a new splinter that will execute a stored payload after a delay. Player-cast splinters must begin within my own ambit and snap to the nearest half-block.$(br2)Inputs:$(br)position: world-space vector to summon at.$(br)delay ticks: non-negative delay before execution.$(br)payload: non-empty list of iotas to store and execute.$(br2)Cost: 5 dust times (active splinters + 1).

Cast only by a splinter. Creates a successor splinter using the current splinter's stored payload and ravenmind.$(br2)Inputs:$(br)position: world-space vector for the successor.$(br)delay ticks: non-negative delay before the successor executes.$(br2)Cost: replacing at the same snapped position is free; moving to a new position costs 0.5 dust.
Cast only by a splinter. Returns the origin vector of the currently casting splinter. Useful for self-renewal, local offsets, and checking whether a successor remained in place.
Dismisses all active splinters owned by the caster.

Creates a smooth, stateful trail channel keyed by id. Each cast appends one new point at position; the prior point is remembered automatically. Segments longer than 10 blocks do not connect.$(br2)Inputs:$(br)position: world-space vector for the new point.$(br)color from: RGB vector (0..1) at the start of the color cycle.$(br)color to: RGB vector (0..1) at the end of the color cycle.$(br)ticks: positive transition duration for color ping-pong.$(br)id: numeric trail channel key; reuse to continue the same trail.$(br)type?: optional style index, rounded and clamped to 0..14 (default 0).$(br2)Types: 0 dust ribbon, 1 sparks, 2 trail, 3 pulse beads, 4 end rod, 5 witch plume, 6 enchant flecks, 7 soul flame beads, 8 solid beam, 9 ring lattice, 10 lightning arc, 11 hex conjure ribbon, 12 hex conjure sparks, 13 hex conjure trail, 14 dual-helix.$(br)Color vectors tint types 0-3 and 8-14; types 4-7 use native particle colors.
hexcasting.page.manifestation.contained_: Planting a villager's mind into a box etched to channel the caster's will creates a Contained Splinter. It is made by flaying a novice villager into a block of slate.$(br2)The Contained Splinter can hold a focus. Insert a focus carrying a hex, and when the circle reaches the block it manifests a splinter and waits for that splinter to finish before the circle proceeds.

While active, the contained splinter casts as part of the circle and uses circle and splinter ambit rules. Using Renew Splinter with Locate Splinter's current position can create unusual self-looping behavior.$(br2)A redstone signal works as an interrupt: it immediately dispels the active anchored splinter and allows the spell circle to continue.
hexcasting.page.manifestation.intents.li: Manifests a Relay linked from a destination vector to a target vector. It manifests on the face of the destination vector cast is looking at. Optionally takes a redstone signal strength (0-15) to emit when the relay is activated.
hexcasting.page.manifestation.intents.un: Clears an existing link from a Manifest Relay block.
hexcasting.page.manifestation.intents.op: A threshold is a boundary where intent manifests. To cross it is to make the caster's will real.
hexcasting.page.manifestation.intents.de: Unravels manifestation blocks in a radius around a vector. Only blocks your will can edit are affected.
hexcasting.mishap.manifestation.menu_ope: Manifestation backlash: this menu is being reopened too quickly.
hexcasting.mishap.manifestation.menu_fro: Manifestation menus cannot be opened from a splinter cast.
hexcasting.mishap.manifestation.radial_m: Radial manifestation only accepts button intents.
hexcasting.mishap.manifestation.intent_r: Manifest Relay cannot manifest on that face; the space is blocked or unsupported.
Manifest Relay signal strength must be between 0 and 15.
hexcasting.mishap.manifestation.portal_n: Manifest Threshold cannot manifest. The space is occupied.
hexcasting.mishap.manifestation.splinter: Contained Splinter requires a focus before the circle can proceed.
